subject: Elon Musk
evidence_standard: L1 = verbatim traceable, L2 = reported verifiable, L3 = inferred

education: University of Pennsylvania — BA Physics, BS Economics (1992-1997)

early_formation.grandfather: Joshua Haldeman — aviator, chiropractor, anti-government far-right activist, alleged Nazi sympathies; told Musk stories of 'adventure and exploration'
early_formation.father: Errol Musk — electromechanical engineer, pilot, entrepreneur; verbally harsh; Musk chose to live with him at age 9 for Encyclopedia Britannica and computer access; later regretted
early_formation.bullying: Hospitalized after being thrown down concrete steps at paramilitary wilderness school; father berated him upon discharge
early_formation.self_taught_programmer: Learned BASIC from VIC-20 manual at age 10; sold Blastar game at age 12 for ~$500
early_formation.science_fiction: Formative books: Lord of the Rings, Foundation series, Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
early_formation.military_avoidance: Emigrated to Canada in June 1989 specifically to avoid South African mandatory military service and apartheid participation

key_facts: Co-founded with brother Kimbal Musk and Greg Kouri
City guide / web software company; secured New York Times and Chicago Tribune as clients
Worked 'seven days a week, all the time' (L2: his own account)
Built plastic supercomputer facade to impress investors (L2: reported)
Exit: Compaq acquired for $307M in Feb 1999; Musk received $22M (7% stake)
Founded X.com 1999 as online financial services; attracted 200,000+ customers in first months
Board replaced him as CEO within months — deemed 'inexperienced'; replaced by Intuit CEO Bill Harris
X.com merged with Confinity (Thiel/Levchin) in 2000; Musk became CEO but preference for Microsoft over Unix caused Thiel resignation
Board removed Musk a second time, installed Thiel as CEO
Company renamed PayPal; eBay acquired for $1.5B in 2002; Musk received $175.8M (11.72% stake)
Pattern: Unable to control direction → removed; capital preserved despite ouster
Founded May 2002 with $100M of personal fortune after two failed attempts to buy Russian ICBMs
First Falcon 1 launch: 2006 (failed); three consecutive failures nearly bankrupted company
Fourth Falcon 1 attempt: 2008 (reached orbit); secured $1.6B NASA contract same year
2012: Dragon vehicle docked with ISS — first commercial spacecraft to do so
2015: Successfully landed Falcon 9 first stage — reusable rocket milestone
2018: Launched Falcon Heavy; payload was Musk's personal Tesla Roadster
Starlink: 7,600+ operational satellites by May 2025 (65% of all operational Earth satellites)
2024: NASA awarded $843M ISS deorbiting contract
Vertical integration: Built engines, avionics, manufacturing in-house — unusual in aerospace

career_timeline.role: Majority investor (2004), CEO and Product Architect (2008-present)
key_facts: Led Series A investment of $6.35M in Feb 2004; company founded by Eberhard and Tarpenning in 2003
Became CEO and Product Architect after Eberhard's ouster in 2008
2009: Designated 'co-founder' via lawsuit settlement despite joining post-founding
Vehicle ladder: Roadster (2008) → Model S (2012) → Model X (2015) → Model 3 (2017) → Model Y (2020) → Cybertruck (2023)
Production Hell (2017-2018): Model 3 ramp catastrophically missed timelines; Musk slept on factory floor
2020: Tesla became world's most valuable carmaker
October 2021: Market cap reached $1 trillion (sixth US company to achieve)
SEC lawsuit (2018): 'Funding secured' tweet for Tesla-private deal → $20M fine + board resignation + tweet restrictions
SolarCity acquisition (2016): $2B for cousins' company over shareholder opposition; 10%+ stock drop on announcement
Brain-computer interface company; aims to integrate human brain with AI
2021: Macaque played Pong via neural implant
Federal welfare probe launched 2022: employees alleged pressure from Musk led to 'botched experiments and unnecessary animal deaths' (L2: reported)
FDA approved six-year human trials September 2023
Underground vehicle tunnel company
Built initial test tunnel on SpaceX property to avoid permits
LA tunnel demo 2018: 'rough ride at suboptimal speeds' (L2: reported)
Las Vegas Convention Center tunnel completed early 2021 — only completed urban tunnel
Chicago and West LA projects canceled — regulatory/permitting barriers more intractable than engineering

career_timeline.role: Owner, CEO (Oct 2022 - May 2023), Executive Chairman / CTO (May 2023-present)
key_facts: Reached 9.2% stake early 2022; made $43B offer in April; completed $44B acquisition October 27, 2022
Immediately fired top executives (CEO Parag Agrawal, others)
Laid off ~50% of workforce within days
Reduced content moderation → documented increase in hate speech and misinformation (L2: reported)
Released 'Twitter Files' on Hunter Biden laptop moderation to delegitimize previous leadership
Rebranded to 'X' 2023
Banned ElonJet account; suspended journalist accounts covering the ban
May 2023: Stepped down as CEO (per poll he ran); became Executive Chairman/CTO — power without formal responsibility
Co-founded 2015 as non-profit AI safety research organization; pledged $1B but donated only ~$50M
Left board 2018: discontent with direction; later claimed was squeezed out
Pattern: Unable to control direction → exited → built competing xAI in 2023
Launched July 2023 as direct ChatGPT competitor
Hired engineers from Google and OpenAI
Became SpaceX subsidiary 2026 (consolidation play)
Grokipedia (October 2025): AI-generated encyclopedia; cited Infowars, Stormfront; content framed 'in line with Musk's own views'

career_timeline.role: Senior Advisor / de facto DOGE leader
key_facts: Appointed by Trump January 2025
White House director sworn statement: Musk had 'no actual or formal authority'; Trump contradicted this
Presided over federal mass layoffs 2025
Senate report: Musk could avoid $2B in legal liability from DOGE actions
Left administration May 28, 2025

key_decisions_and_what_they_reveal.decision: Founding SpaceX with $100M personal fortune after Moscow ICBM failures
key_decisions_and_what_they_reveal.reveals: Willing to self-fund from first principles when institutional path closed; not deterred by 'experts say no'

key_decisions_and_what_they_reveal.decision: Pushing through three Falcon 1 failures in 2006-2008
key_decisions_and_what_they_reveal.reveals: Iteration-as-strategy; accepts early failures as learning data points; doesn't treat failure as final

key_decisions_and_what_they_reveal.decision: Sleeping on Tesla factory floor during Model 3 'production hell' 2017-2018
key_decisions_and_what_they_reveal.reveals: Personal presence as signal to team; willingness to share operational pain; manufacturing complexity genuinely surprised him

key_decisions_and_what_they_reveal.decision: Acquiring Twitter for $44B in 6 weeks from 9.2% stake
key_decisions_and_what_they_reveal.reveals: Impulsive action at scale; narrative control more important to him than financial return; personal vision overrides board/shareholder logic

key_decisions_and_what_they_reveal.decision: SolarCity acquisition for $2B over shareholder opposition
key_decisions_and_what_they_reveal.reveals: Vertical integration instinct; personal/family interests influence corporate decisions; believes his judgment > market judgment
Public statements used as power tool; consequences of regulatory friction underestimated; tweets are operational decisions

key_decisions_and_what_they_reveal.decision: Exiting OpenAI board and founding xAI
key_decisions_and_what_they_reveal.reveals: Consistent pattern: when unable to control direction of important technology, builds competing alternative

key_decisions_and_what_they_reveal.decision: Making Starlink a $10B commitment without guaranteed revenue model
key_decisions_and_what_they_reveal.reveals: Capital commitment follows strategic vision, not proven demand; treats large bets as inevitable if physics allows

documented_failures_and_learnings.specifics: Board removed him twice — first for being 'inexperienced', second for technical preference (Microsoft vs Unix) overriding business logic
documented_failures_and_learnings.learning_demonstrated: Technical genius ≠ organizational leadership; later compensated by taking CEO roles himself and building board deference

documented_failures_and_learnings.specifics: Three consecutive failures nearly bankrupted SpaceX and drained personal reserves; fourth succeeded
documented_failures_and_learnings.learning_demonstrated: Aerospace iteration is slower and more expensive than software; rapid failure requires more capital buffer than anticipated

documented_failures_and_learnings.specifics: Manufacturing ramp catastrophically missed timelines; overreliance on automation caused problems; Musk moved to factory floor
documented_failures_and_learnings.learning_demonstrated: Manufacturing complexity is fundamentally different from engineering complexity; 'the machine that makes the machine' is the hard problem

documented_failures_and_learnings.specifics: Promised 'free speech' platform; result was documented spike in hate speech, antisemitism, and misinformation
documented_failures_and_learnings.learning_demonstrated: Content moderation is not censorship — it's infrastructure; deletion without replacement creates externalities

documented_failures_and_learnings.failure: Neuralink animal deaths from pressure-driven acceleration
documented_failures_and_learnings.specifics: Federal welfare probe 2022; employees documented 'botched experiments and unnecessary animal deaths'
documented_failures_and_learnings.learning_demonstrated: Speed-over-safety in biological systems produces irreversible damage unlike reversible engineering failures

political_and_personal_context.political_evolution: Bipartisan donor (Obama 2008/2012, Clinton 2016, Biden 2020) → Republican post-2022 → explicit far-right support 2023-2025 → largest individual donor in 2024 US election ($50M+ to Citizens for Sanity PAC)
political_and_personal_context.turning_point_documented: Biden's 2021 EV executive order excluded Tesla; Musk not invited. GM CEO Mary Barra said in 2024 Musk 'never forgave' the snub (L2: reported). Kamala Harris later called the snub 'a big mistake'
political_and_personal_context.international: NBC News (Feb 2025): documented Musk boosting far-right movements in 18+ countries since 2023
political_and_personal_context.note: Political evolution documented for board dynamics context, not ideological endorsement
